desc:
Here, in a crown high above the other tree tops, the foliage is crawling with
spiders spinning their small webs. Dozens of dark butterflies flutter about
here, looking for nectar. As far as the eye can see, the forest spreads out
endlessly in all directions. Only to the south can a small line be seen marking
where the road is.
name: Top of a Sprawling Black Yew

desc:
Dark vines cling to low branches in this gloomy forest. In the shade of the
trees, bushes and ferns thrive, as though the lack of light does not bother them
at all. To the south and west the forest and dense foliage continues without
reprieve.
name: Stygian Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
A grove of twisted oaks, with an almost malevolent aura about them, rise up
amidst thorny thickets and sprawling bushes. To the north, a craggy outcrop of
dark rock thrusts up suddenly through the forest floor. It is covered in
deep-red flowers that give it the appearance of a broken, uneven row of
monstrous blood-soaked teeth.
name: Stygian Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
Old and gnarled ash trees grow here, watching over a forest landscape overgrown
with thorny bushes. There is enough space between these gloomy sentinels to
squeeze through in single file. A faint odour of decay and putrifying plants
hangs in the air, refusing to give way for weather or wind.
name: Stygian Shadows beneath a Dark Canopy

desc:
A strange band of unidentifiable mottled trees encircle a small patch of grass.
Long vines hang down from above and lie in tangled coils on an ancient,
petrified stump in the centre of the small clearing. Around and beyond the
strange grove of trees, Mirkwood awaits, gloomy and veiled in shadows.
name: Shifting Forest

desc:
The lower end of vines sway gently in the trees here, like some strange creature
waiting for a hapless victim to wander into its trap. It is difficult to see
further than half-way up the canopy as the higher parts are hidden in gloom. The
trees seem to be leaning slightly to the west, where it looks like the forest
grows darker and more ominous.
name: Stygian Darkness within the Forest

desc:
The ground slopes slightly downwards here forming a valley in the forest. At the
bottom of the valley, there is a huge black elm standing in lone majesty. Its
branches seem to rustle threateningly as if warning away creatures or indeed the
very forest itself from approaching.
name: Stygian Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
An impressive and solitary fir tree stands here, resolute against the
surrounding forest of twisted, corrupted trees. It gives the impression of the
last soldier, surrounded by a tainted force. The largest boughs of the fir are
spread on an east-west axis like arms, and the powerful roots are drawn up
slightly out of the ground as though it has been preparing itself to flee from
assailants.
name: Stygian Darkness within the Forest

desc:
The ancient trees are covered by layers of long, grey lichen which makes the
forest appear as if there are hundreds of gnarled, hunched figures with long
beards standing in the gloom. The lichen sways slowly in the wind, and vines
dangling from the branches above caress the ground. Most of the vines have
natural, green shades, but one species stands out for its inflamed red colour.
This vine winds itself tightly around some of the trunks. The impression is that
of ongoing strangulation, and although the effect might be an illusion, the
trees suffering the red vines seem less hale than the rest of the forest.
name: Stygian Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
In this havoc of untamed, shifting vegetation, it is unlikely that any permanent
paths or trails can be found. Even as an opening between thorny bushes seems to
materialise, it begins to disappear again. There is a constant background noise
of faint but distinct hissing sounds.
name: Stygian Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
This huge linden tree is very easy to climb, because of the thick and rough
bark. The linden starts branching at low height with several very thick branches
that can easily be reached from the ground, which makes the climb even easier.
In one branching point, there is an almost flat area. Apparently some creatures
have found the place and made it their home, since there is a nest made of
pieces of vine, hay, and other vegetation in there.
name: Tree-top Nest

desc:
The forest and undergrowth have created a mayhem of gnarled trunks, thorn-clad
branches, entangled vines, and dense bushes. Paths through the undergrowth seem
to appear only to vanish instants later, as the foliage shifts in ways no wind
could accomplish. The forest floor is barely visible and these woods are perfect
hiding places for any manner of wild animal that slithers or crawls.
name: Dead-end in a Stygian Wood

desc:
The bed of the gully is wet with mud and decaying leaves, and the air is filled
with the stench of putrifying plantlife. Vicious-looking, thorn-covered bushes
line the steep ridges of the gully, their thick branches leaning out above as if
threatening to leap down into the gully and attack trespassers.
name: Shallow Gully in a Stygian Forest

desc:
Ashes and oaks, gnarled with age, loom overhead, lining the sides of this
fissure. Fiercely tangled brambles, thorns gleaming with dew, obstruct the way
north. The ground continues to slope gently downwards to the south as the
twisting fissure disappears from view. On the ground, a narrow crack between
cliff and rocks is visible and there is a passage down into darkness.
name: Dark Fissure in a Stygian Forest

desc:
The trees seem to form a natural cavern-like room from apparently dead branches,
vines, and other vegetation. The decaying vegetation has interwoven into a sort
of curtain preventing almost any sunlight from shining through. Thorny shrubs
and thickets below the trees complement the curtain of decaying vegetation,
perfecting the area.
name: Stygian Darkness within a Decaying Forest

desc:
Oak and ash, interspersed with an occasional dark alder, spread out in all
directions. A powerful malignant aura seems to encompass these woods. The trees
look unhealthy and are spotted with patches of mould and strange growths. Vines
dangle from branches above, trembling with inner life and shifting about as if
they were tendrils. The ground is unusually soft and covered in rotting leaves,
absorbing the sound of footfalls.
name: Stygian Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
This channel is formed in the rock deep beneath Mirkwood. There is a bit of
moisture on the ground, but the water is not flowing. The surface is smooth and
corners are rounded by something probably many years ago. The rounded tunnel
continues to the east, while its other end to the south has collapsed, leaving
piles of rubble on the floor of the tunnel and dark openings in the ceiling
above. The air is cool and humid here, dozens of feet beneath the forest floor
of Mirkwood.
name: Underground Channel

desc:
This underground channel, is embedded in the rock dozens of feet beneath
Mirkwood. The tunnel leads off in three directions. The walls have been
smoothened by something long years ago, perhaps water, and a flora of lichen and
fungi grows on the walls of the tunnel.
name: Fork in a Channel

desc:
This is a cavern with smooth walls and there is a very narrow trough in the
western wall. The trough is not big enough to enter. There is a crevice in the
ceiling to the north. Its surface is smooth and slippery, but the walls are not
very steep, so it's easy to traverse there. A thin layer of stagnant water
gathers on the bottom of the channel, but it is muddy and there is too little
for a drink. Sound echoes strangely here in the rocky channel.
name: Underground Channel

desc:
This tunnel has smooth walls, and a large bowl-like depression in the floor
suggests that water fell from above. There is an unreachable fissure in the
ceiling. A pile of leaves and branches lie in the depression where they've
fallen through, and from the sides of the fissure, roots can be seen poking
through the walls.
name: Underground Channel

desc:
An outcrop of broken rock rises from the forest floor but even at its pinnacle
the outcrop does not break through the canopy of Mirkwood. Wide branches from
the surrounding forest intertwine above and hide the sky from view. Seregon
covers much of the dark rock, its blood-red flowers rising up from the clefts
like a wound in the rock itself.
name: Shattered Outcrop in a Stygian Forest

desc:
The forest here is filled with dense tangles of thorny bushes and thickets. The
ground is a maze of roots snaking around each other, and most of the trees are
overgrown with vines. The vines seem to be of a strange sort with rows of
needle-like thorns growing along their lengths.
name: Stygian Shadows beneath a Dark Canopy

desc:
The forest thins out to the east and west where dark groves of alder trees keep
the undergrowth to a minimum. To the east, a jagged outcrop rises from the
forest floor, making movement in that direction seem difficult. Decaying vines
hang limp in the shadow of the outcrop like the tentacles of a thousand
long-dead octopi.
name: Stygian Shadows of an Ominous Forest

desc:
Dark trees rise high in the gloom of their own canopy, from which a plethora of
vines reach down in odd, intertwined patterns. The impression is that of a
gigantic cobweb made of plantlife, each silken thread of the web as thick as a
wrist. The undergrowth of ferns and thorny bushes is dense and inhospitable,
although a perfect habitat for snakes and lizards, and anything else that
slithers or crawls.
name: Border of a Fell, Stygian Forest

desc:
The aged trees that dominate most ancient forest are largely absent here. Only a
few scattered oaks, ancient and gnarled sentinels, remain standing. Between
thorny bushes and those few sprawling ancients, a vast number of saplings grow.
The young trees grow in odd shapes, leaning and twisting against and around each
other as if involved in a physical struggle.
name: Deep within a Fell Forest

desc:
The forest here is hidden in shadows and gloom. The canopy above is impenetrable
and the undergrowth consists mainly of ferns and other plants that need little
light. There is an open area, free of other trees and plants, around a twisted
black tree, as if it consumes the light with such absoluteness that nothing else
can survive near it. Though no greater in stature than the other trees, it seems
to loom over them as the queen of this region of Mirkwood.
name: In the Shadow of a Fell Tree

desc:
The forest here is completely overgrown and moving through it is almost
impossible. Especially the thickets and bushes, equipped with sharp, black
thorns, hinder movement. The branches of the ancient oaks growing here shift and
rustle in the wind, but sometimes louder noises are heard as unseen animals move
through the undergrowth.
name: Fringe of a Fell, Stygian Forest

desc:
The thorns on the thickets here are black and sharp. From the tips of the
thorns, there is some sort of liquid seeping out. The undergrowth is almost
impassable, although it is slightly less dense to the west, where the crowns of
several ancient, sprawling oaks block the sunlight with such finality that only
smaller ferns grow under them. In the bushes and thickets, there's the
occasional rustling sound as something moves through it unseen.
name: Tangled Maze in a Fell Forest

desc:
The forest growth has become a mayhem of entangled branches, vines, and
thorn-clad bushes. The mere act of walking ten feet in a straight line presents
an almost impossible challenge. Thick, gnarled roots crawl across the ground,
largely hidden underneath the foliage.
name: Thorns and Brambles in a Fell Forest

desc:
The canopy of Mirkwood allows for some glimpses of sky between boughs and
leaves. Even so, not even the brightest summer day could lift the shadows that
rest over these woods like a suffocating blanket. The trees, ancient and
gnarled, are chiefly a mix of oak and aspen with the odd sprawling linden
interspersed with them. High ferns thrive in the space between the tree trunks
and the forest floor is completely obscured.
name: Edge of a Fell, Stygian Forest

desc:
Towering ancient oaks dominate the forest here. Some of these have stood rooted
in the same spot for thousands of years. Massive, gnarled roots crawl across the
forest floor. The largest are thick and high enough to conceal a pony standing
on the other side. Few species of plants grow beneath the dense foliage of the
oaks, but these are ideal conditions for the ferns that thrive here.
name: Fell Oaks within a Shadowy Forest

desc:
This part of Mirkwood is overrun by infernal bushes reaching more than ten feet
high. They are covered in wicked thorns and form thick walls that completely
obscure the other side from view. What might be narrow paths can be seen in some
places, although it is impossible to say if they lead anywhere. A lifetime could
be spent being scratched and torn while trying to find a way out of here.
name: Tangled Maze in a Fell Forest

desc:
In this corner of the forest, there are a lot of broken branches and fallen
trees on the ground. Something huge has crashed mindlessly through the forest,
not caring about neither the thorny bushes nor the trees. The forest seems to be
passable only to the west, although the ever-shifting foliage makes it hard to
see a clear path.
name: Dead-end in a Stygian Wood

desc:
A wide variety of trees thrive here, growing in an entangled chaos as they
compete for the sun's light. There is a small pond in the centre of the area,
nestled in-between looming trunks. Several corpses of unidentifiable creatures,
badly decayed and falling into pieces, float in the water and swarms of flies
hover above them. A strong odour of death and decay fills the air, and at times
one of the corpses is jerked about as if something in the pool was tugging at
them.
name: Outskirt of a Fell, Stygian Forest

desc:
The willows in this part of Mirkwood are twisted and bent in ways that only a
sick, malevolent mind could possibly envisage. The trunks scrape and chafe
against each other, with some looking as if they would fall over if not for
being deeply entangled in the dense canopy above. Thick vines are wrapped about
trunks and branches, giving an impression of ropes that shackle the trees to
keep them from escaping.
name: Among Twisted, Fell Willows

desc:
This part of Mirkwood is an entanglement of bushes, twisted willows, and thorny
briars. Only a narrow path is visible to the north through the forest.
Occasional rustling noises are audible, originating from somewhere deep in the
forest. Some of the willow branches, along with the thorns of the briars, have
brownish stains upon them which resemble dried blood.
name: Deep inside a Fell, Stygian Forest

desc:
The forest here is largely birch. They might have been ordinary trees once, but
now they are gnarled and withered. Their branches rustle and the twisted trunks
creak dangerously whenever approached as if the forest itself is aware of
intruders. The ground is covered by ferns, tangled brambles and round,
moss-covered boulders.
name: Thorns and Brambles in a Fell Forest

desc:
This part of the forest is completely overgrown by stunted, deformed willows.
The trunks bend and twist in ways that should be impossible for a tree. The
canopy is very dense, leaving only hints of a sky visible. Beneath this foliage,
only ferns thrive, but as if to compensate, they do so in abundance.
name: Thorns and Brambles in a Fell Forest

desc:
Enormous thorny bushes grow everywhere. They are so sprawling and entangled with
one another that it is impossible to say where one bush starts and the next one
begins. Few trees grow here, but those that do have huge crowns, large enough to
blot out most of the sky and cast the bushes into perpetual gloom. Rustling
noises are ever-present deep in the bushes; an aura of malevolence and the
forebodings of imminent doom seem to hang heavily in the air.
name: Thorns and Brambles in a Fell Forest

desc:
Twisted elms stand huddled together in groups around this hollow. Shrouded in
shadows with crowns of mottled green, they lean inwards creating an enclosed
circle-like area inside the forest. The lair between the elms is at least a
hundred feet across and overgrown with tall bushes. Bloated, distended cadavers
that have started to putrefy, hang from the lower branches. Strewn amidst the
razor-sharp thorns, crumbling husks and the dismembered skeletal remains of
long-dead creatures litter the area.
name: Fell Lair
